# Executive Technology Brief

**Date:** 2026-06-23
**Execution Time:** 03:00 AM (Local Company Time)
**Report ID:** ETB-2026-06-23-001

**Generated By:** Technology Intelligence Routine — NT Executive Technology Office

---

## 1. Executive Snapshot

- **Claude Fable 5's free grace period ends today.** From June 9–22, Anthropic included Fable 5 (Mythos-class, state-of-the-art on nearly every benchmark) in Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans at no extra cost. Starting today, June 23, it is premium-only at $10/$50 per million input/output tokens. Any team that piloted Fable 5 workloads in the last two weeks must now cost-model those workloads under full pricing or revert to Opus 4.8. Audit Fable 5 usage and decide immediately.

- **SpaceX IPO closed at $75B — largest in history — with xAI absorbed into the company.** SpaceX listed on Nasdaq (SPCX) at $135/share on June 12, raising $75B and opening at $150. xAI (Grok) was already merged into SpaceX in February. The real strategic asset is not Grok (downloads down 60% since January) but Colossus — the world's largest GPU cluster, which Anthropic is renting for $1.25B/month. Infrastructure economics, not model superiority, is now the decisive competitive lever in AI.

- **MCP (Model Context Protocol) crossed into enterprise-grade territory with a new authorization layer.** The protocol now has 97M+ monthly SDK downloads and 9,600+ registered servers. The previously missing enterprise requirement — authorization enforcement — was added by Anthropic, AWS, Microsoft, and OpenAI maintainers in joint coordination. MCP is no longer a developer experiment; it is becoming infrastructure. Any internal AI tooling built without MCP compatibility will require a refactor within 12 months.

- **Andrej Karpathy joined Anthropic from OpenAI to lead a team accelerating pre-training research.** This is a marquee talent acquisition: Karpathy is the most credible AI researcher-communicator in the industry. His move from OpenAI to Anthropic is both a talent signal and a market signal — the ecosystem's gravitational center is shifting. It also validates Anthropic's technical ambitions beyond "safe Claude" into frontier research leadership.

- **EU AI Act high-risk enforcement is 40 days away (August 2, 2026).** Penalties for non-compliance with Annex III high-risk AI systems reach €15M or 3% of global turnover. Most enterprises remain unprepared — over half lack complete AI system inventories. Any AI feature touching EU users in employment, credit, education, health, or biometric contexts is legally exposed. This is not a future risk. It is a current countdown with a fixed deadline.

## 2. Technologies Worth Watching

---

### Technology 1: Claude Fable 5 — Mythos-Class Model Now at Full Pricing

**Category:** Foundation Models / AI Infrastructure / Cost Strategy

**What happened?**
Anthropic released Claude Fable 5 on June 9, 2026 — its most capable model ever made publicly available. The model is described as "Mythos-class made safe for general use," achieving state-of-the-art performance on nearly all tested benchmarks including FrontierBench (highest-scoring coding eval) and breaking 90% on complex long-running analytical tasks — a 10-point jump over Opus 4.8. Key specs: 1M token context window by default, up to 128K output tokens per request, $10/$50 per million input/output tokens. As of June 23 (today), the free-for-paid-plans grace period ends. The model handles multi-file code, PDFs with embedded charts, and extended autonomous task execution.

**Why it matters?**
The transition from grace period to paid pricing is a forcing function for every team that experimented with Fable 5 in the last two weeks. Teams that relied on it in production now face a cost decision. More strategically, Fable 5 sets a new capability baseline: if your AI pipelines were built around Opus 4.8 or Sonnet 4.6 assumptions, Fable 5 may enable tasks that were previously not feasible (1M-token context, true multi-step agentic sustained tasks). The model also ships with conservative safety filters that trigger in < 5% of sessions — this is the new standard for "Mythos-capable but enterprise-deployable."

**Business Value:**
(1) Audit all internal Fable 5 usage that began during the grace period — identify which workloads justify the premium pricing vs. which can return to Opus 4.8. (2) Identify one or two high-value tasks where the 1M token context window unlocks something impossible before (full codebase analysis, large document ingestion). (3) Model Fable 5 API cost vs. Opus 4.8 for our primary Claude workloads before defaulting to the new model.

**Company Relevance:** 92 / 100

**Priority:** High

**Recommendation:** Evaluate with immediate cost audit. Identify the one workload where Fable 5's extended context and analytical depth provides clear ROI justification. Run a 2-week cost comparison before committing to it as a default model.

**Expected Impact:** Short-term (cost decision required this week; capability upside: immediate)

---

### Technology 2: MCP Enterprise Authorization Layer — Protocol Becomes Infrastructure

**Category:** AI Integration Standards / Developer Infrastructure / Enterprise AI

**What happened?**
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) reached a critical enterprise maturity milestone: Anthropic, AWS, Microsoft, and OpenAI jointly added an enterprise authorization enforcement layer — the single requirement that blocked serious enterprise adoption. Current scale: 97M monthly SDK downloads, 9,652 registered servers, and 41% of surveyed software organizations already in limited or broad production deployment. The 2026 MCP Roadmap published by the MCP Foundation outlines standardized transport protocols and enterprise SSO/RBAC integration as H2 targets. MCP is now on the top-five technology priority list for most software organizations.

**Why it matters?**
MCP is becoming the TCP/IP of AI agent connectivity — a universal standard for how AI models communicate with external tools, data sources, and services. The authorization layer addition removes the last enterprise-grade blocker. Any internal tooling, data pipeline, or service that doesn't expose an MCP interface will increasingly require manual integration work as AI systems proliferate. The window to build MCP-native first is closing; within 12 months this will be a table-stakes requirement, not a differentiator.

**Business Value:**
(1) Any internal APIs or services we expose to AI agents should be designed with MCP in mind from now on. (2) Evaluate which third-party tools we use that have published MCP servers (Supabase, Stripe, GitHub, Notion all have them) — these can be connected to our AI agents without custom integration code. (3) The enterprise authorization layer means compliance-sensitive AI agent workflows (HR, finance, legal) are now a realistic buildout target.

**Company Relevance:** 88 / 100

**Priority:** High

**Recommendation:** Adopt as integration standard. Begin an MCP server inventory of tools we use. Designate one internal service to receive an MCP wrapper as a pilot within 30 days.

**Expected Impact:** Medium-term (12-month adoption horizon; foundational decision to make now)

---

### Technology 3: NVIDIA RTX Spark — AI-Native PC Chips Enter the Market

**Category:** Hardware / Edge AI / PC Ecosystem

**What happened?**
At Computex 2026 on June 2, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang unveiled the RTX Spark — a new laptop chip designed to run AI workloads locally at performance levels previously requiring cloud infrastructure. Announced alongside Microsoft in a "reinvent the PC" joint positioning. The chip targets premium devices first, with mid-range options following. NVIDIA is simultaneously pursuing a multi-layer strategy: cloud (H100/B100 data center GPUs), edge (RTX Spark), and open models (Nemotron, Cosmos, Alpamayo, Isaac GR00T, Clara families).

**Why it matters?**
Local AI execution is transitioning from a privacy feature to a performance feature. RTX Spark-class machines can run models and inference workloads without network latency or cloud cost. For organizations running developer machines, the delta between a developer on an RTX Spark laptop vs. a developer on standard hardware in terms of AI-assisted productivity will be measurable within 12 months. NVIDIA's cross-stack presence (hardware, CUDA, open models, enterprise SDKs) is increasingly difficult to avoid.

**Business Value:**
(1) Engineering team hardware procurement cycles should factor in RTX Spark availability when targeting 2027 upgrades. (2) Local inference capability enables private AI workflows (sensitive data that cannot leave the device/network) without cloud cost. (3) NVIDIA's open model releases (Nemotron agentic family) are free to deploy — evaluate for internal tooling before defaulting to proprietary APIs.

**Company Relevance:** 68 / 100

**Priority:** Medium

**Recommendation:** Observe now. Add to 2027 hardware planning. Evaluate NVIDIA Nemotron open models for specific internal agentic tasks where proprietary API cost is prohibitive.

**Expected Impact:** Medium-term (hardware cycle: 12–18 months; open models: testable now)

---

### Technology 4: MiniMax M3 — First Open-Weight Model With Frontier SE + 1M Context + Multimodal

**Category:** Open Source AI / Foundation Models / Competitive Landscape

**What happened?**
MiniMax released M3, described as the first open-weight model combining frontier-tier software engineering capabilities with a native 1-million-token context window and multimodal computer use (vision-language-action). Built on the MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) architecture. This is a significant open-weight milestone: previous open models either had strong code performance OR large context OR multimodal capability, but not all three simultaneously.

**Why it matters?**
The gap between open-weight and frontier proprietary models is narrowing faster than expected. M3 changes the build-vs-buy calculus for AI infrastructure: any workload requiring large-context code analysis, document processing, or multimodal reasoning now has a self-hostable alternative. For teams with data privacy requirements or high API volume, M3 removes the "we must use a proprietary model" constraint for a class of tasks. The open-source AI ecosystem is now producing models that compete with paid APIs on specific capability dimensions.

**Business Value:**
(1) Evaluate M3 for any workload where we currently pay for large-context API calls at volume. (2) For privacy-sensitive tasks (internal code review, sensitive document analysis), M3 offers a self-hosted path. (3) The combined frontier SE + 1M context is directly applicable to codebase analysis use cases.

**Company Relevance:** 72 / 100

**Priority:** Medium

**Recommendation:** Pilot. Run M3 against our most context-heavy API workloads and benchmark against Opus 4.8 for cost-adjusted quality. Time investment: 3–5 days to set up and evaluate.

**Expected Impact:** Medium-term (cost optimization available within 30–60 days if evaluation validates)

---

### Technology 5: Andrej Karpathy at Anthropic — Pre-Training Research Acceleration

**Category:** AI Research / Talent Signals / Competitive Intelligence

**What happened?**
Anthropic hired Andrej Karpathy from OpenAI. Karpathy is founding a team focused on using Claude to accelerate pre-training research — meaning Anthropic is now applying its own best model to improve how it builds future models. This is a recursive improvement loop: Claude-assisted research accelerating the next Claude.

**Why it matters?**
Karpathy's move is the highest-signal talent acquisition in AI since Demis Hassabis built DeepMind. He carries credibility with the developer community (3.5M+ Twitter followers, creator of micrograd/nanoGPT, architect of Tesla Autopilot's neural stack) in a way that directly affects enterprise and developer trust in the Anthropic ecosystem. His research focus on pre-training suggests Anthropic believes the next capability leap comes from training methodology, not just scale — a thesis worth tracking. For anyone building on Claude APIs: the talent concentration at Anthropic is now exceptional.

**Business Value:**
Indirect but high. (1) Anthropic's research trajectory strengthens the long-term viability of Claude as our primary AI platform partner. (2) Karpathy's public communications about what Claude can and cannot do will be highly credible and worth monitoring. (3) This signals that Anthropic will continue to compete at the frontier, not just in "safe AI" positioning.

**Company Relevance:** 70 / 100

**Priority:** Medium

**Recommendation:** Observe. No immediate action required. Monitor Karpathy's public outputs for early signals about where Claude's next capabilities are heading.

**Expected Impact:** Long-term (research → product cycle: 12–24 months)

## 3. New Tools & Platforms

---

**Tool:** Cloudflare Flagship

**Category:** Cloud / Feature Flags / Edge Infrastructure

**What does it do?**
Native feature flag service built into Cloudflare's global edge platform. Evaluates flags locally in Cloudflare Workers — no external flag service calls, no latency overhead. Built on OpenFeature standard. Currently in closed beta.

**Who should use it?**
Engineering teams using Cloudflare Workers who currently pay $50K–$150K/year for LaunchDarkly, Statsig, or similar feature flag SaaS. Also any team wanting to consolidate feature flagging with their CDN/edge layer.

**Business Use Case:**
Replace dedicated feature flag SaaS with infrastructure-native flags. Zero external API calls during flag evaluation = zero latency penalty. Rollout control, A/B experiments, and kill switches without leaving the Cloudflare ecosystem.

**Estimated ROI:**
High if currently paying for a standalone flag service. Moderate for teams on free/starter flag tiers. Eliminates one SaaS line item.

**Recommended Experiment:**
If using Cloudflare Workers for any edge function, apply for the Flagship closed beta and evaluate for one non-critical feature flag within 30 days.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** 1 week

---

**Tool:** Notion Workers

**Category:** Productivity / Developer Infrastructure / Internal Tooling

**What does it do?**
Hosted runtime for custom code embedded directly in Notion. Deploy code via CLI; runs in a secure sandbox. Extends Notion workflows without managing your own servers.

**Who should use it?**
Product and operations teams who want to automate Notion-based workflows (CRM, project tracking, reporting) with custom logic that currently requires external Zapier/Make.com automations or a separate server.

**Business Use Case:**
Internal data processing, custom report generation, Slack-to-Notion syncing, automated status updates, invoice tracking — any workflow currently requiring an external automation layer can be collapsed into Notion directly.

**Estimated ROI:**
Moderate. Reduces external automation tool dependencies. Value is highest for teams heavily invested in Notion as their operational OS.

**Recommended Experiment:**
Identify one current Make.com or Zapier automation that touches Notion. Rebuild it as a Notion Worker. Compare reliability, latency, and cost.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** 2–3 days

---

**Tool:** GitHub Copilot Sandboxed Execution

**Category:** Developer Tools / AI Coding / Security

**What does it do?**
GitHub Copilot can now execute code inside secure, isolated sandboxes — both locally and in cloud environments — for tool execution (tests, builds, linting) without touching the host system.

**Who should use it?**
Any engineering team using GitHub Copilot for code review, PR automation, or agentic coding tasks who needs isolation guarantees for AI-executed code.

**Business Use Case:**
Enables Copilot to run tests, validate suggestions, and execute refactors autonomously in an isolated environment. Removes the risk that AI-generated code execution touches production state during development.

**Estimated ROI:**
High for teams doing AI-assisted code review at scale. Sandboxed execution removes the primary security objection to agentic coding in enterprise contexts.

**Recommended Experiment:**
Enable Copilot sandbox execution for one engineering team's PR review workflow. Measure time-to-review and defect escape rate over 3 weeks.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** 1 week setup, 3-week measurement window

---

**Tool:** Supabase Passkeys (Beta)

**Category:** Authentication / Security / Developer Infrastructure

**What does it do?**
Passwordless, phishing-resistant authentication for Supabase Auth via WebAuthn. Users sign in with biometrics, device PINs, or security keys. Currently in beta.

**Who should use it?**
Any product team using Supabase Auth who wants to eliminate password-based authentication for their users or internal tools.

**Business Use Case:**
Replace password auth with passkeys for any user-facing app or internal tool built on Supabase. Phishing resistance is particularly high value for admin and sensitive-access paths.

**Estimated ROI:**
High for security posture. Negligible cost (included in Supabase). Reduces support overhead from password resets.

**Recommended Experiment:**
Enable passkey auth on one internal tool or staging environment within 2 weeks.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** 2–3 days

---

**Tool:** MAI-Code-1-Flash (Microsoft, via GitHub Copilot)

**Category:** AI / Coding Models / Developer Tools

**What does it do?**
Microsoft's small-tier coding model, rolling out inside GitHub Copilot. Designed for fast, low-cost code completion and generation tasks where Copilot's heavier models are over-provisioned.

**Who should use it?**
Engineering teams that find GitHub Copilot's latency too high for inline completions, or organizations managing Copilot API costs at scale.

**Business Use Case:**
Accelerates inline code suggestions without burning expensive inference compute. Acts as a first-pass model in a cascade (MAI-Code-1-Flash for simple completions → larger model for complex reasoning).

**Estimated ROI:**
Cost reduction on Copilot usage at scale. Speed improvement for inline completion UX.

**Recommended Experiment:**
No explicit setup required — rolling out to Copilot users automatically. Monitor change in completion latency and acceptance rate over the next 30 days.

**Estimated Evaluation Time:** Passive observation over 30 days

## 4. Global Technology Direction

### Where is the market moving?

**The compute layer is being acquired, not built.**
The defining strategic move of June 2026 is not a model release — it is Anthropic paying SpaceX/xAI $1.25B/month to rent the Colossus GPU cluster. The world's best AI lab cannot build or buy compute fast enough to match its demand. Infrastructure scarcity is real, and the companies that control physical GPU capacity (NVIDIA, Meta, SpaceX/xAI, Amazon, Microsoft, Google) have a structural advantage that compound over time. AI companies are becoming infrastructure tenants, not owners.

**Models are becoming commoditized at every tier except the frontier.**
Claude Fable 5 is Mythos-class capability at public API pricing. MiniMax M3 is open-weight with frontier SE capabilities. MAI-Code-1-Flash is free inside Copilot. The middle of the model market is collapsing. Competitive advantage is shifting to: (1) which model is at the absolute frontier (Fable 5 / Mythos territory), and (2) which model is cheapest for a given accuracy threshold. There is no strategic value in using a medium-tier model anymore.

**MCP is consolidating the AI integration layer.**
The addition of an enterprise authorization layer to MCP signals that the protocol has cleared the final enterprise adoption barrier. Anthropic, AWS, Microsoft, and OpenAI all maintaining it jointly is unprecedented — it means MCP is not Anthropic's protocol, it is the industry's protocol. This is analogous to REST becoming the web standard: resistance is futile and early adoption is advantaged.

**Agentic coding is crossing the 50% threshold.**
95% of engineers use AI weekly. 75% use AI for half or more of their work. 55% use AI agents regularly. Claude Code is the most-used tool at 46% — more than double Cursor at 19%, more than five times GitHub Copilot at 9%. This is not a trend — it is a shift in baseline operating assumptions for engineering teams.

---

### What This Means For Our Company

1. **We are in the middle of a capability transition, not a stable plateau.** The models we use today will be significantly cheaper and more capable within 90 days. Cost-model our AI infrastructure defensively — what we are paying today is likely not what we will pay in Q4.

2. **MCP is the integration standard to bet on.** Every internal service we want to connect to AI agents in the next 12 months should have an MCP interface. Building point-to-point integrations is now technical debt before it is written.

3. **Agentic coding tools are a productivity arbitrage.** If our engineers are not at 46%+ Claude Code adoption, we are paying human-time rates for tasks that our best-adopting peers are solving in minutes. This gap compounds daily.

4. **EU AI Act August 2 deadline is the most immediate legal exposure.** Regardless of company size, any AI system touching EU users in high-risk categories must be audited, documented, and classified. The fine scale (up to 3% of global turnover) makes this a CFO-level risk, not only a technology decision.

## 6. CTO Decision Board

### START THIS WEEK

1. **Run a Fable 5 usage audit.** Identify every workload that used Claude Fable 5 during the June 9–22 grace period. For each: (a) does it justify $10/$50 per million tokens? (b) if not, revert to Opus 4.8 today before the invoice accumulates. Deadline: end of day June 24.

2. **Initiate EU AI Act compliance audit.** Assign a named owner. First deliverable: complete inventory of all AI systems touching EU users, classified by risk category (prohibited, high-risk, limited-risk, minimal-risk). Deadline: July 7 (leaves 3.5 weeks for remediation before August 2).

3. **Enable Supabase Passkeys on one internal tool.** This is a 2–3 day implementation with zero additional cost and immediate security value. Any admin panel or internal dashboard is the right starting point.

### EVALUATE THIS MONTH

1. **Declare MCP as the internal AI integration standard.** Schedule a 2-hour architecture review. Inventory which tools we use that already have published MCP servers (GitHub, Supabase, Stripe, Notion, Figma all do). Identify the first internal service to receive an MCP wrapper.

2. **Pilot MiniMax M3 vs. Opus 4.8 cost comparison.** Run M3 on our most token-heavy Claude workload for 1 week. Evaluate cost-per-task vs. quality. Decision threshold: if M3 performs within 15% of Opus quality at 40%+ cost reduction, it warrants deeper investment for privacy-sensitive or high-volume workloads.

3. **Measure engineering team AI tool adoption rate.** Survey the team: what percentage use Claude Code, Copilot, or Cursor for 50%+ of their work? Compare against the industry 46% Claude Code benchmark. Identify the adoption gap and plan a structured accelerant (pairing sessions, prompt library, use case demos).

### IGNORE FOR NOW

1. **NVIDIA RTX Spark hardware procurement.** Premium devices only today; mid-range options are 6–12 months out. Monitor but no decision required before Q4 hardware planning.

2. **SpaceX/xAI (Grok) integration or investment thesis.** Grok downloads are down 60% since January. The real xAI asset is Colossus compute infrastructure — Anthropic, not us, is the customer for that. No direct action.

3. **Cloudflare Flagship feature flags.** Still in closed beta; not worth disrupting current flag tooling until GA. Apply for beta access now but plan evaluation post-GA (estimated Q3 2026).

## 7. Trend Detection

Comparing against reports from June 13–21:

---

**Trend:** MCP as the AI integration standard is accelerating from developer-facing to enterprise-mandatory

**Confidence:** High (repeated in 6 of the last 8 reports; now backed by enterprise authorization layer addition by all major AI providers jointly)

**Suggested Action:** Move from "observe" to "adopt as standard." This trend has crossed the confidence threshold — acting after 12 more months of evidence is waiting too long.

---

**Trend:** Anthropic ecosystem gravitational pull continues to increase

**Confidence:** Very High (Karpathy hire, $965B valuation, Seoul expansion, Fable 5 GA, Colossus rental deal — 5 consecutive signal reinforcements in 2 weeks)

**Suggested Action:** Formalize Anthropic as primary AI platform partner in our internal AI strategy documentation. Conduct a dependency audit: where are we using Claude and what is our exit plan if needed?

---

**Trend:** Open-weight models are closing the gap with frontier proprietary models on specific capability dimensions

**Confidence:** Medium-High (MiniMax M3, NVIDIA Nemotron, Meta Llama evolution — consistent progression; gap still exists at absolute frontier)

**Suggested Action:** Add a quarterly open-weight model evaluation into our AI stack review process. Do not wait for open-source to be "obviously better" — run comparative tests before the market prices in the gap.

---

**Trend:** EU AI Act enforcement deadline creating a compliance urgency spike across enterprises

**Confidence:** High (August 2, 2026 is a fixed date; over 50% of enterprises are non-compliant per CSA research; 40 days remain)

**Suggested Action:** Immediate. This trend has a hard deadline. See CTO Decision Board above.

---

**Trend:** Agentic coding adoption approaching inflection point — tools are moving from assistants to autonomous operators

**Confidence:** High (55% of engineers use AI agents; multi-agent coding teams announced by Goldman Sachs, Walmart, BMW; Claude Code at 46% market share)

**Suggested Action:** Treat engineering productivity benchmarking as a board-level metric. Establish a baseline AI adoption rate for our engineering team this month and set a 90-day improvement target.

## 8. Strategic Roadmap

### THIS MONTH (June 23 – July 23)

- **Day 1:** Fable 5 usage audit — identify and cost-model all grace-period workloads
- **Day 1:** Assign EU AI Act compliance audit owner; begin AI system inventory
- **Week 1:** Enable Supabase Passkeys on one internal tool
- **Week 2:** Enable GitHub Copilot sandboxed execution for PR review workflow
- **Week 2:** MCP architecture review — select internal API integration standard and identify pilot service
- **Week 3:** Begin MiniMax M3 cost comparison pilot vs. Opus 4.8 on highest-volume workload
- **Week 3:** Complete EU AI Act AI system inventory (pre-July 7 deadline)
- **Week 4:** Survey engineering team AI tool adoption rate; set 90-day target

### NEXT 90 DAYS (July 23 – September 23)

- Complete EU AI Act risk classification and publish internal compliance posture before August 2 enforcement date
- Build and deploy first internal MCP-wrapped service; publish internal MCP integration guide
- Evaluate MiniMax M3 results; decide on open-weight model policy for privacy-sensitive or high-volume workloads
- Conduct Fable 5 structured evaluation: identify the 2–3 internal tasks where 1M-token context or extended agentic execution justifies premium pricing
- Target 50%+ engineering team AI-assisted workflow adoption; run pairing sessions and internal Claude Code use case library
- Monitor Cloudflare Flagship for GA announcement; begin feature flag consolidation planning if GA by August
- Review Anthropic API pricing trajectory and model pre-IPO contract scenarios (Anthropic IPO: October 2026)

### NEXT 12 MONTHS (June 2026 – June 2027)

- **AI infrastructure:** All major internal services expose MCP interfaces; AI agents can reach any internal system with proper authorization
- **Model strategy:** Clear policy for frontier vs. efficient vs. open-weight model tiers by workload type; quarterly open-weight model evaluation cadence
- **Engineering productivity:** Track and report AI-assisted productivity as a core engineering metric; target 2–3x velocity improvement on qualifying task types vs. June 2026 baseline
- **Compliance posture:** Full EU AI Act compliance documented and audited; AI governance framework covering model selection, data handling, and incident response
- **Hardware:** Factor RTX Spark / local inference into 2027 engineering machine procurement; evaluate for privacy-sensitive workloads
- **Agentic operations:** At least one multi-agent system in production (e.g., code review agent + test generation agent + security scan agent operating as a coordinated team on PRs)
- **Anthropic IPO watch:** By October 2026, assess whether post-IPO API pricing changes require contract renegotiation or multi-model diversification acceleration
